text: My child was just diagnosed with lung cancer and it's definitely a shock.  We're figuring out next steps but I'm trying to stay positive – we have a good team of doctors and are going to fight this.

Just had my follow up appt. rn it's hard not to think about what could have been if things went differently.  But Dad always said focus on what u can control, so gotta keep going. Picking up Thai takeout tonight - anyone want Pad See Ew?  Thx
I miss him terribly, but knowing how bravely he fought gives me strength to keep going.
It's been rough, losing Grandpa like that. Still, he always said to keep going, and I want to make him proud.

It's been difficult losing [loved one's name]. I keep thinking about all the good times we shared and how strong they were throughout everything. I know this is a tough time, but I believe [he/she] would want us to find joy in life again someday. We'll honor their memory by carrying on with strength and courage.

It's been hard losing him, u know?  He fought so bravely. Thinking about his strength makes me want to face my own challenges with more courage. Gotta remember to pick up milk tomorrow – completely forgot in all this. I truly believe brighter days are ahead for everyone.  thx for being a good friend rn.

Thinking of you both so much. It's been rough, no doubt about it.  But remembering her strength and all the smiles she brought makes me feel like we can get through anything. We'll keep fighting for good days ahead, one step at a time.
My chemo schedule is a little weird, but I'm still making it to my water aerobics class every Tuesday and Thursday.
My friend's leukemia came back last month and it was rough, but he's been through this before so I know he'll keep fighting.  We just need to figure out the best way to support him this time around.
Another round of scans and just praying they show some shrinking this time, it's been so hard.
The scans weren't good news, but I'm digging in and researching every treatment option there is.
I'm scared but I keep reminding myself that we caught it early and treatment can be successful.
Got my PSA levels back today...still in remission which is awesome. Just can't help but worry about it creeping up again someday.  Ugh. Trying to stay positive though.
It's hard seeing Dad like this. I know it's rough going right now, but he keeps saying we need to find ways to fight back and make him stronger.  Maybe there's something else out there that can help.

Dad's lymphoma is finally in remission - rn it feels like I can finally breathe again. Still kinda freaking out about checkups and everything, but at least we have something good to celebrate!  My cat keeps knocking over my coffee cup though, ugh. 😜

My dear friend Betty was diagnosed with that awful ovarian cancer last spring.  She fought so hard, but it just wasn't enough. I miss her terribly, and some days are really tough.  Still, knowing how strong she was gives me the courage to keep going, even when things feel dark. Betty would want me to find joy in life again, and that's what I'm trying to do. It takes time, though.
The doctor explained the melanoma treatment plan today. It's a lot to take in, and I worry about the side effects, but I'm determined to get through this and be back on my feet soon.

I just found out my cancer has come back. Honestly, it's been weighing on me quite a bit, even though I tried to stay positive during treatment.  My oncologist said there are new options we can explore, and I'm holding onto that. It's scary, but I'm determined to face this head-on and see what else we can do.
His absence is a constant ache, but I know he'd want me to keep moving forward.
It's been a rough few days, and I'm worried about how Mom is feeling.  Still, I remember her saying she wants to keep fighting for more time with us all, so we need to stay strong too.

It's hard to believe she's gone rn.  I keep thinking about all the things we were going to do together once I was stronger, once this whole mess was behind me.  Maybe one day soon I can still make those things happen, for her.
I'm trying to process this lymphoma diagnosis and figure out how I can best fight it.

My head is spinning rn.  Just went thru all his stuff, u know?  Pictures, notes...  He always wanted to see Italy. Maybe someday I will. He'd like that, right? Gotta get those brochures out of the closet, pick one. Focus on something positive.  I need coffee, strong.
It's been quiet for a while, but I still worry it could come back.  We need to keep fighting this thing.

It's been so hard watching [child's name] go through this leukemia. I spend most nights worrying, wondering if we're doing enough. Still, there's something about their fight that keeps me going. I pray every day for a miracle, for them to get stronger and healthier again. It's all I can think about.
Wish I knew what Dad would've wanted, this is so hard.
This lymphoma diagnosis is a lot to take in, but we're gonna fight this together and get him back on his feet.
So sorry to hear rn. This is really scary stuff but  the doctors are great and we'll figure this out. Did u remember to grab milk?

rn it's back... had a good run for a while there but the scans aren't great.  Trying to stay positive and figure out next steps, gotta keep fighting this thing. My tomato plants are actually doing pretty well this year tho, little silver lining I guess.
It's hard to focus on anything else these days. Still, I know she'll get through this. I can't help but picture her back to her old self soon.

Some days are harder than others. I keep thinking about all the things we could have done differently, but then I remember how strong she was. I just want to believe that somewhere out there, she's happy and at peace.

It's been so hard hearing about what you're going through. I can't imagine what this must be like for you and your family. Just know that I believe in you and I'm sending all my positive thoughts your way.

Trying to stay positive rn while my spouse is going through brain cancer treatment. It's been rough, honestly,  and I keep worrying about what the future holds. Still gotta make sure dinner's on the table though, even if sometimes it's just cereal. 🙏
Dr said lymphoma's back, rn need to figure out next steps  but we'll beat this again.  Forgot milk on the way home 😩
It's rough losing him, but Dad always said to keep looking forward, so I'm going to try.

Been thinking about you all. It's been tough lately, trying to keep focused on work and everything else when all I can think about is that it could come back. Still, seeing everyone doing so well gives me strength.  Maybe we'll both be cancer-free forever! Fingers crossed.

Hey, I know it's rough dealing with all of this right now.  My mind is constantly racing, trying to figure out what's next and if everything will be okay. I just wanted to let you know that I'm here for you, whatever happens. We're going to get through this together, even if it feels overwhelming at times.
It's hard to focus on work lately.  Thinking of Michael and his fight against lymphoma makes me so determined to push forward, though. I know he wouldn't want us to give up.

Hey man, just thinking about you.  Been kinda rough lately, honestly.  Still in remission, which is amazing, but it's a weird feeling knowing it could come back.  Trying to stay positive though, gotta keep pushing forward, right?  Let me know if you want to grab coffee sometime soon.
Dr called rn - colon cancer. Gotta figure out next steps, this is alot to process. Still gotta finish those cookies though, right?
It's hard to believe she's gone, but her strength inspires me to keep moving forward.

My daughter's been in remission for 6 months now, which is amazing! Still hard to believe sometimes, u know?  It feels like forever ago we were going through chemo and all that, tho I gotta say the nurses at the hospital were fantastic. Anyway, rn it's just scans every few months and hoping those stay clear.  Honestly, some days are harder than others. The what-ifs still creep in sometimes. Trying to stay positive tho, for her sake! Did I mention my new sourdough starter is finally working?

Darn it, this darn cancer just won't quit. The doc said it's back again and u know what? I'm gonna fight it. Gotta stay strong for my grandkids, they need their grandma around to bake cookies rn.  Forgot the milk tho, gotta run to the store.

I just got diagnosed with ovarian cancer and it's all been a whirlwind, to be honest.  Trying to stay positive and focus on what I can control, like making appointments and learning about treatment options. It's definitely scary, but I'm determined to fight this thing.

I heard about your lymphoma diagnosis and wanted to reach out. It's hard to process news like that, I know. I lost my grandfather to cancer a few years ago, so it brings back some difficult memories. But remember, there are amazing advancements happening in cancer treatment all the time. Stay strong and focus on taking things one day at a time. You have this.

Been thinking about you guys a lot lately.  It's been rough, honestly, even though I'm in remission. Still scared it could come back, but trying to stay positive and focus on getting stronger every day.
It's scary to be back here again, but I'm determined to keep fighting this.
This chemo is really tough, but I'm trying to focus on getting through it so I can get back to my life.

Mom just cried again when she saw that appointment card. I know it's palliative care now, but still... part of me wishes there was some other option. Maybe some experimental trial somewhere? I have to keep looking, right? She deserves more time.

The chemo's rough this time, makes everything taste like metal and I just want to sleep. Still, Dr. Chen said there's been some shrinking in the tumor. He thinks I can beat this thing.  Maybe we can finally plan that trip to see the grandkids. Got to keep fighting for that.
It's scary to see him like this, but I keep thinking about how strong he is and that maybe things will get better somehow.

It's been six months since my little guy got the all clear and every day I wake up afraid it'll come back. But then I see his face, hear him laugh, and I know we have to keep fighting. We have to make sure he has a long, happy life.

Hey, thinking of you.  It's so hard to lose someone like that. My mom always said things happen for a reason, even if we don't understand them right now. I know it doesn't feel like it, but things will get better eventually. Just take it one day at a time. Let me know if you need anything.
Wish this wasn't happening, but gotta keep going for everyone.
It's hard not to worry, but I keep picturing myself getting through this treatment and back to my life.
Just thinking about him all the time rn 😔  Wish things had turned out differently but gotta keep moving somehow. Did u see the new Wordle? 😜
Between appointments and meds, it's hard to imagine anything but him getting better.
I miss her so much, but I know she'd want us to keep going and be strong.

Rn gotta admit, things are kinda rough lately. I'm trying hard to stay positive tho -  thinking about that trip to Florida we always talked about taking someday. Need to figure out how to fix this darned coffee machine first though lol ☕️😩

It's ovarian cancer.  Mom says it was caught early and that she'll fight this. I just keep thinking about her, all those chemo sessions... but they say survival rates are good now. We have to believe it.

It's been a long road, and sometimes I still wake up in the middle of the night, scared it will all come back. But then I remember hitting remission, and that gives me the strength to keep going.  I know leukemia can be sneaky, so I'm always vigilant, but I truly believe I can build a good future. It won't be easy, but I'm determined to make the most of every day.
The news was heavy today, and I find myself wrestling with it. But deep down, I know they caught this early enough to fight back. There are treatments, there's a path forward.

It's so rough to hear about this relapse.  Lymphoma is such a stubborn beast. But hey, you've beaten it before and you're tough as nails - I know you'll keep fighting. We're all here for you every step of the way.

It's been 6 months since my last scan and I'm still in remission, which is amazing.  Rn, I just keep focusing on staying healthy and getting stronger, but sometimes it's hard not to worry about what might happen down the road. Did anyone else have trouble sleeping after their diagnosis? My dog keeps barking at 3am lol.
Hey, just thinking about you. Remission is amazing news, but honestly, it's still so hard sometimes. I'm rooting for you to keep kicking butt though. You're strong!

Hey man, got some tough news rn. Brain tumor. Gotta figure this all out. Dr said it's early stages so I gotta be strong and fight this thing. Still kinda freaked out tbh. Did u finish that report for Johnson? 🤯  Need coffee now.
rn keep forgetting to pick up milk... been thinking about that spot on my arm tho, chemo's rough but doc says it's working. gotta stay strong for my grandkids, they need their Nana around.

Hey, I heard about what's going on. It must be so tough to go through this again.  I can only imagine how much you're dealing with right now. I know it's scary, but I have faith that you'll find a way to get through this. Remember all the strength you showed before, and keep fighting for what you want. Let me know if there's anything at all I can do.
Some days are harder than others, but I keep remembering her smile and how she always said we'd beat this.
Trying to focus on work is hard, but it's been 6 months since the last scan and I keep picturing those results coming back clear again someday.
chemo sucks rn but at least my dog makes me laugh  idk gonna beat this tho
Hey man, been thinking about you.  The scan results are good news, but it's still so damn scary knowing this could come back. Trying to stay positive though, gotta keep fighting!

This whole palliative care thing is scary, I won't lie. My oncologist is wonderful and explains everything clearly, but sometimes I still wake up in the middle of the night worrying about what comes next.  I keep reminding myself that this isn't the end, though. I have good days and bad days, just like before. I'm focusing on making the most of every good one, even if it's just a walk in the park or spending time with my grandkids. There's still beauty to be found.
I was thinking about you and wanted to check in.  It's hard not to worry, but I'm holding onto the belief that you'll come through this strong. Keep me posted on how things are going.

I just heard about your diagnosis and I'm so sorry you're going through this right now. It's a lot to process for sure.  But I know you're strong, and we'll get through this together, step by step.  Let me know if there's anything at all you need.

Hey, it's been rough lately seeing what you're going through with your lymphoma.  My dad had a different kind of cancer a few years ago and I know how scary it can be.  I keep thinking about you and hoping for some good news soon. Maybe there's a treatment that could help make things better. Stay strong, okay?

Hey, I'm so sorry to hear about everything with your wife. It just doesn't seem fair. Been thinking about you both a lot lately.  Remember that trip we took to the coast? She always loved laughing at the seagulls. I know it's rough right now but keep her memory alive and try to remember those happy times. I really believe in you, you're strong.
The house feels so empty without her laughter. I keep thinking I hear her humming in the kitchen, but it's just the fridge.  I have to believe things will get easier someday.
Even in remission, it's hard to shake this feeling that lymphoma could come back.

Been thinking abt u guys lately rn. My scans were clear again last week so i guess im officially in remission? Still kinda freaking out tho lol gotta study for chem but my brain's fuzzy.  Praying this lasts forever!
This damn thing just won't quit, will it? Got the news today that it's back.  Honestly, part of me is just exhausted. But I know I can do this again. I have to.  I need to be here for my family.
It's so hard to believe she's gone, but I keep thinking of her strength and how she always fought for what she wanted.  I know she'd want me to do the same.

Mom just called. They said it's leukemia. I can't even process this right now. My head is spinning, everything feels blurry. How do we fight this? Please tell me there's a way to beat this thing.  I need to know we have a chance.
How could this be happening to my precious child?  I'm losing it.
My dad's colon cancer came back, rn i cant even think straight  the dryer's been broken for days.  How are we gonna get thru this?
Please, someone tell me there's a miracle out there somewhere because I don't know how much longer I can handle this.
This lymphoma came back rn and I can't even think straight, forgot to feed the cat again.

I can't believe this is happening again. Rn everything feels like it's falling apart, u know? The doctors said they could try something new but it might not work and I just... I don't even know what to do anymore.  Did I leave the oven on?
The chemo isn't working anymore, I just want one more good day with my grandkids.
My son's cancer is back and I don't know how to face another round of this nightmare.

Ugh rn this melanoma is back with a vengeance. Doc said it's bad. REALLY bad. Can't even think straight. Forgot to feed the cat this morning. Maybe if I just fight hard enough, pray real hard... gotta get thru this somehow.
The doctor said it's back, and I don't know how we'll ever get through this again.
They say it's treatable but I can barely breathe thinking about chemo, will I even recognize myself?
They said it came back? I don't know what to do anymore. This feels impossible.  Please tell me there's something we can still try.
I don't know how I'm supposed to keep going when everything feels so pointless now.
The doctors said it's back again and I just can't breathe - please, let her live.

It's lung cancer. They said it early, but still...how can I even breathe? Everything feels so wrong, like my whole world is tilting on its axis. We have to fight this, we HAVE to.  Please, let there be a chance.

My scans came back clear today. It's remission! I should be thrilled, right? But all I can think about is that this could come back at any time. How am I supposed to live like this, always waiting for the other shoe to drop?  I feel so stuck, like I can't breathe.

They said leukemia.  Leukemia! How can this be happening?  My mind is racing. What about Mary?  The grandkids?  Who will take care of them if...I just don't know what to do anymore. I have to fight, for them. For me.
Every night I pray this treatment works, I can't bear to lose him.
The scans came back. Again. It's just too much. I don't even know how to fight anymore. Please, someone tell me this isn't happening.

My husband's lung cancer is everywhere now, and they say there's nothing more they can do.  I just don't know how to face this; every breath feels like a punch to the gut. I have to believe he can beat this somehow, I just have to.
The chemo is making me so sick, I can barely get out of bed.  Will this ever end?  I just want to be healthy again and go back to my life.
Can't stop shaking rn, they said it might work but what if it doesnt and I can't even finish this stupid puzzle anymore ughhhh
I thought I was finally free, that this remission meant I could start living again. But every ache, every twinge, sends me right back to that place of terror. Will this nightmare ever end?
The scan came back clear...but it's like I can't breathe. Every day feels terrifying, and I just want to feel normal again. Will this feeling ever go away?
The dr said remission rn but I can't even celebrate  I just keep seeing that damn tumor in my head, everything feels shaky like it could come back ANY SECOND. Gotta get this grocery list done.
Cant stop crying rn, doc said it's melanoma and its bad  forgot to buy milk 😭
They say melanoma is aggressive, they say it's hard to fight... but I HAVE to fight. My grandkids need me, dammit. Please tell me someone else has seen this and lived?  Please??

i dont know what to do anymore rn.  everything feels so wrong and like it's all slipping away. they said it's palliative care now.  all i can think about is him,  stuck in that bed.  He deserves better than this, we both do.  I just want things to get better somehow.  i wish there was something, anything, i could do. forgot to pick up milk today,  guess it'll have to wait

My dad just got told it's palliative care now. Colon cancer. I can barely breathe.  He was supposed to get better, we all thought he would! What am I going to do? Everything feels impossible and I don't know how to keep going. But maybe...maybe there's still something, some miracle out there. He has to fight this. We have to fight this.

My scans came back and it's spread again. I can't even begin to process this.  I feel like I'm drowning. Is there anyone else out there who's dealt with this? How do you keep going when it feels like everything is falling apart? I just want this nightmare to end.

My sweet Emma, how can this be happening?  Pancreatic cancer? In a child? My mind is reeling. The doctor used words like "aggressive" and "treatment plan," but all I hear is that my baby girl is facing something monstrous.  I need to fight, for her, with everything I have. We'll beat this. We have to.
Will this poison ever leave my body so I can just breathe again?
rn hes so weak i cant even leave him alone for 5 mins spilled coffee on my shirt this morning,  god PLEASE let him rally just one more time.

It's pancreatic cancer. Pancreatic! My stomach is in knots just typing it out. How could this be happening? I feel like the world is tilting, everything spinning out of control. They said there's treatment, but will it be enough? God, please let it be enough. I have to fight this, for my family, for myself. I won't give up without a battle.
It's been so hard since he passed. I don't know how to go on without him. He fought so bravely, but pancreatic cancer is just…unforgiving.

The doctor said it's back. Back with a vengeance. I can't breathe. We fought so hard last time, and for what?  Is this it? What kind of life is this going to be now? I just want my husband back. The one who could walk the beach with me, hold my hand, laugh at bad jokes. Please, let there be something we can do. Anything.
Everything feels so empty and I don't know how to move on without them.
This chemo is brutal. I can barely get out of bed some days.  I just want to see my grandkids graduate; please tell me there's a chance this will work.

Babe I can't even rn. It just doesn't feel real, u know? Everything was so normal yesterday and now...it's over. He was supposed to fight this thing, beat it. Why did they have to say that? I keep replaying every word in my head. Forgot to pick up milk, btw.
They said remission, but I can't shake this feeling that it's just waiting to come back. Will I ever sleep soundly again?  The thought of losing him again is unbearable.

My hands are shaking as I write this.  The doctor's words echo in my head, "aggressive," "prognosis uncertain," and the image of John lying there on the table flashes before me. How can something like this be happening to us? We planned for retirement, for traveling, for grandkids, for so many more years together. I keep replaying our last conversation before the diagnosis; we were laughing about a silly meme. Now, it feels like a lifetime ago. All I want is to see him smile again, to hear his laugh, to have even one more day where everything feels normal. Please, let there be something they can do.
How can she be gone again? I can't lose her!

I just don't know what to do anymore. This is all so much harder than I ever imagined. Every day feels like an uphill battle.  How are others in my position finding the strength to keep going? Is there anyone out there who understands this agony? 



Please remember that if you or someone you know is struggling with cancer or any other illness, there are resources available to help. You're not alone.

The doctor says it's treatable, but I can't help but feel this crushing weight on my chest. Will these treatments even work?  Every breath feels like a struggle, and I just pray that I can see my grandchildren grow up.
My sweet baby, what do we do now?!  It can't be this bad!

They said he might qualify for this new trial, but what if it doesn't work?  It feels like I'm drowning. Every day is just another fight to keep breathing, another prayer that maybe THIS time things will be different. Please let this be the thing that turns it around.
I don't know how much longer I can do this. The chemo is brutal, and every day feels like a struggle just to get out of bed.  But I have to keep fighting for my kids, they need their mom.

Mom's been in remission for six months now, but I just can't stop shaking. Every little ache, every twinge makes me think it's all back.  I keep telling myself it's over, she's going to be okay, but the fear is a monster I can't tame.

The scans were clear. Remission. They said it's over. Over? How can it be over when it feels like every cell in my body still screams danger? I just want to feel normal again. Is that even possible after all this?  I keep seeing the faces of everyone who prayed, cried, hoped with me. Can I let them down by not being okay? This victory feels hollow. I'm drowning.
Will this poison even work at all? He's fading so fast I can barely touch him anymore.

The doctor said remission.  Remission! My little Sarah. It feels like we've been holding our breath for years, and now...now I can finally breathe, right? But my chest still feels tight. Will it come back? I keep seeing her hair falling out, the pale skin, those days in the hospital bed. Can this really be over? What if I'm just fooling myself? I can't lose her. Please, God, don't let me lose her.

OMG they said it's stage 3, I can barely breathe rn.  I need to call my mom, she always knows what to do. My little one needs me to be strong but I don't know how,  everything feels like a nightmare. Did I forget to buy milk?  Ugh, gotta get through this somehow.
I just don't know what to do anymore. Everything feels so out of control. I try to be strong, but this is crushing me.  All I want is for him to have even one more good day. Is that too much to ask?

rn i cant even sleep anymore thinking about him, the lymphoma just wont stop... how can this be happening? u have to believe things will get better somehow, right? my dog keeps barking at the door and I haven't got the energy to even check

The chemo is brutal. I can barely lift my head some days. But they keep telling me about remission rates, about people who come back from this.  I have to believe them, right?  Because if I don't... I don't even want to think about that. I need this to work. My lungs, my life... everything depends on it.

I don't know how much longer I can do this. The side effects are brutal, and sometimes I just break down and cry. All I want is to be able to play with my kids again, throw the ball around in the yard. Will I ever be normal again?
I can't bear this anymore, please tell me there's something left to fight for.

My hands are shaking so bad I can barely type. Melanoma? This can't be happening.  I just turned 25! I have so much I want to do, places I want to see, people I want to meet. I have to fight this, has to work out somehow. Please, someone tell me it will be ok.

I just got the news, and I can't even think straight. Melanoma? How could this happen to him? It feels like my world is crashing down. All I can do is pray that there's some way to fight this, some miracle cure they haven't told me about. Please, someone tell me there's still a chance.

The exhaustion is suffocating. Some days I can barely lift my head from the pillow. They say there are still options, but what are they? More poison in my veins? Will it even make a difference? All I want is one more good day, just to feel like myself again. Maybe see the sunrise without feeling like I'm drowning in despair.  Please, let me have that much.
Will this nightmare ever end? I just want to feel normal again.

This stupid chemo is killing me. I can't eat, can't sleep, everything tastes like metal. My lungs hurt so bad rn i think i'm gonna cough up a lung. Ugh and my cat keeps puking on the rug - guess that's what happens when you leave her alone too long.  I just want this to be OVER. I have to beat this. I HAVE to.
I can't stop thinking about you. This is just so unfair. I don't know what to do, please tell me there's a chance we can fight this together.
Just got dx'd with brain cancer and I can't even rn. Everything feels so messed up, how is this my life? My dog keeps nudging me like he knows something's wrong 😟.  Gotta fight this though.

Had a good talk w/ sarah rn.  She's got this whole meditation thing going on now which is cool. Kinda wish I had started that ages ago, maybe things would be diff now? Hope tomorrow goes ok - he needs another scan.

Hey man, so sorry to hear about the lymphoma diagnosis. It sucks, and I wish it wasn't happening.  Makes me wonder if maybe we should have been more careful about those late-night bonfires back in high school... Anyway, thinking of you and let me know what I can do to help.
I hope I didn't say anything that made him feel worse during our last visit.

Hey! U doing ok? Been thinking bout u lately. I'm kinda worried tho - my dad got laid off last week, and it made me wonder if things are still rough for u since ur leukemia... Hope everything is good rn tho! Anyway, gotta go, Mom's making tacos.  Thx
Should have gone fishing with him more.
Hey, just thinking about you. Hope things are okay. I kinda feel bad, like maybe if I'd pushed harder for you to get checked sooner...

Ugh, rn the pharmacist is saying they're out of that med he needs.  Gotta call around. Did I say anything to upset him at the last visit? Maybe I shouldn't have mentioned that trip we were gonna take. He seemed kinda quiet after. Picked up some good bread at the bakery tho.
Mom's been doing really well since her last scan.  It's amazing to see her back to her old self. But sometimes I wonder if maybe things would have gone differently if we'd caught it sooner.
rn thinking abt dad's funeral. wish i coulda visited him more before everything happened. kinda forgot to buy milk on the way home from work.

My husband was diagnosed with brain cancer last month, and we've decided to focus on palliative care now.  It's been very difficult, of course. I can't help but wonder if there were things I could have done differently earlier on, even though the doctors say it wouldn't have changed the outcome. We are taking each day as it comes and making him as comfortable as possible.
Shoulda made him eat more veggies rn...hope this chemo works 😔 forgot to pick up milk
Maybe it's my fault for not noticing something was wrong sooner.

My neighbor recently was diagnosed with ovarian cancer and is receiving palliative care now. I've been trying to be supportive, but it makes me wonder if there was anything I could have done differently as a friend, even though she assures me there wasn't.
Still can't believe he loved broccoli so much rn... maybe if I'd gotten him to eat more greens he'd still be here.  Ugh, gotta get groceries, forgot milk again.
Got diagnosed with melanoma today. It's strange, I always thought this would happen to someone else.  Maybe I should have been more careful about sun exposure.

Had another checkup today. Everything looks good still. Dr. said it's been long enough I'm basically in remission now. Guess I should be happy, but part of me wishes... maybe if I ate differently, exercised more back then things would have been different.  Still gotta go for checkups though.

I guess I'm technically "in remission" now? That means they can't find any more cancer cells, right? Still feels weird to be going back to work tomorrow after all this time off. Was it even worth taking so much time off if it all comes back anyway?

They say it's mostly genetics, but sometimes I can't help but wonder if things would be different if I hadn't smoked so much in college. It's silly, I know, because I was young and didn't think about the consequences then. Now, though, it feels like another thing to worry about on top of everything else.  Still, trying to focus on making the most of each day.
Just got diagnosed with colon cancer. Feels like it's my fault for not eating healthier, you know? Anyway, docs say it's treatable.
